Title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 2, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed higher on Thursday, supported by gains in the telecommunications sector and a rebound in heavyweight banking stocks.
Investor sentiment remained restrained due to uncertainty on the Federal Reserve's policy direction.
The ringgit, meanwhile, strengthened against the US dollar at 4.0720.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded higher at RM246,617.
Following the trend, Ethereum traded higher at RM6,607, while Solana stood at RM319.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia reaffirms counter-terrorism commitment at UN High-Level Conference
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Home Ministry has strengthened Malaysia's commitment on the global stage through the active participation of the country's delegation in the Fourth United Nations (UN) High-Level Conference on Countering Terrorism at the UN Headquarters in New York.
The ministry said that the Malaysian delegation, led by deputy secretary-general (Security) Datuk Abdul Halim Abdul Rahman, also delivered the country's official intervention statement during Session III: The Global Terrorism Threat Landscape.
"During the session, Abdul Halim firmly presented Malaysia's strategic views on the dynamics of today's security threats.
"This not only underscored the ministry's credibility but also demonstrated Malaysia's role in advancing an inclusive and progressive national security narrative that is aligned with the principles of Malaysia Madani at the international level," it said in a statement today.
It said Abdul Halim also delivered Malaysia's National Statement at the UN General Assembly Hall in New York yesterday under Agenda Item 118: Report of the UN Secretary-General on the Ninth Global Counter-Terrorism Strategy.
"The session highlighted Malaysia's unwavering commitment to supporting multilateral efforts to combat extremism.
"At this highest platform, he outlined the country's holistic approach, aligning domestic security strategies with the global framework and emphasising the importance of international cooperation centred on justice and lasting peace to ensure the long-term effectiveness of counter-terrorism efforts," it added.
It said that the ministry's Public Security and Order Division secretary, Datuk Zulkifli Abidin, and its principal assistant secretary, Elina Roslim, also shared Malaysia's expertise as global panellists during Side Event 16.
The side event, themed "Localised and Whole-of-Society Approaches in Preventing and Countering Violent Extremism", saw both officials present Malaysia's successful practical modules on building youth resilience, strengthening community partnerships and the process of social reintegration.
The conference brought together world security leaders to strengthen strategic leadership and promote a multi-stakeholder approach towards a future free from the threat of terrorism.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media Prima hosts TNB delegation to strengthen ties
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Media Prima Bhd today hosted a delegation from Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) as part of a corporate visit aimed at strengthening the longstanding relationship between the two organisations.
The TNB delegation was led by its chief executive officer, Datuk Shamsul Ahmad, while Media Prima was represented by its group managing director, Datuk Rafiq Razali; chief executive officer of Media Prima Content & TV Networks and Primeworks Studios, Nini Yusof; and group managing editor of Media Prima News and Current Affairs Division, Jasbant Singh.
The delegation was given an overview of Media Prima's operations and its ongoing digital transformation, including the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) and automation across its media platforms.
Shamsul described the visit as an eye-opening experience and praised Media Prima's efforts in embracing technological innovation.
[[nid:1478819]]
"Media Prima plays a very important role in shaping the industry and delivering information effectively to the public. I am impressed by the group's proactive adoption of the latest technologies, including AI and automation.
"I congratulate Media Prima for taking bold steps to drive innovation and position the organisation for future growth," he said.
He said the visit reflected the close ties between TNB and Media Prima, adding that both organisations would continue to strengthen their longstanding relationship and explore opportunities for future collaboration.
"We have enjoyed a long-standing relationship with Media Prima and look forward to building on this partnership in the years ahead," he said.
During the visit, the delegation toured Media Prima Audio, the TV3 and WHI studios, REV Media Group, and KLiK's newsroom operations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Grants, not loans: Ministry push to convert RM100mil TVET fund to ease trainee burden
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry is seeking Cabinet approval to convert a RM100 million allocation for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) financing under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) from loans into grants to ease the financial burden on trainees.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the allocation, announced under the government's TVET 2.0 agenda, was originally intended as a loan facility for eligible trainees, but the ministry hopes it can instead be channelled as grants.
"Currently, it is structured as a loan, but we will try to convert it into a grant.
"I cannot promise, but we are working on it. We will prepare a Cabinet paper and try to convince the prime minister that these grants are necessary for those who genuinely need them," he told reporters after launching the National TVET Instructors and Accredited Centre Managers Conference 2026 today.
Ramanan said the proposed grants would ease the financial burden on trainees, many of whom have to leave their jobs temporarily to pursue skills training.
"Many trainees have to leave their jobs to attend these programmes. If they also have to repay a loan afterwards, it becomes an added burden," he said.
In May, the government announced the Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employability (Pace) Programme,
a RM100 million initiative
managed by PTPK to expand access to skills training and strengthen social protection for workers.
The fund supports 10 programmes aimed at expanding TVET opportunities, accelerating digital economic transformation, strengthening local enterprises, promoting regional development, and improving the employability of gig workers and other underserved groups.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran warns US, Israel against attacks ahead of funeral processions for Khamenei
Article:
DUBAI: An Iranian military commander warned the United States and Israel on Thursday against any attack on Iran as it prepares for the state funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in airstrikes on the first day of the war.
"We warn the enemies of Iran, especially the US and the Zionist regime (Israel), to avoid any miscalculation and to think about the harsh retaliation our armed forces would make to any threat and aggression against our country," Ali Abdollahi, commander of Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, said in a statement carried by state media.
Funeral processions for Khamenei will begin on July 4 in Teheran and conclude on July 9 with his burial in his hometown of Mashhad, with additional ceremonies planned in Qom and Iraq in between those dates.
On Wednesday, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi gave a similar warning that Teheran would deliver an immediate and powerful response to any threat against its people or leadership after comments by Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz that Iran's current Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was "marked for death".
Iranian media reported heightened security measures during the funeral period, while the head of Iran's Civil Aviation Organisation said on Wednesday that temporary airspace restrictions would be implemented over several cities including Teheran and Mashhad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiping elephants gaining weight, remain healthy at Tennoji Zoo
Article:
TAIPING: The Taiping Municipal Council has assured the public that its three elephants housed at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan, are in good health and adapting well to their new environment.
Council president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said the latest veterinary reports and daily monitoring by the Japanese zoo showed that Kelat, Dara and Amoi were active, healthy and receiving proper care.
He said the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry, the Department of Wildlife and National Parks Peninsular Malaysia (Perhilitan) and the council would continue to closely monitor the elephants' welfare, health and management.
"Based on the latest report received from Tennoji Zoo, the condition of all three elephants is positive and they are in good health.
"Veterinary observations and daily monitoring show that the elephants are active, healthy and adapting well to their new habitat," he said in a statement today.
Akmal said all three elephants had also recorded weight gains since the previous update, reflecting progress from continuous health monitoring, dietary management and ongoing care.
He said Kelat gained 260kg, while Dara and Amoi gained 35kg and 30kg respectively.
The elephants are given a balanced diet tailored to their nutritional needs under the supervision of the zoo's veterinary team.
Their daily feed includes hay, bamboo shoots, fresh grass, cabbage, specially formulated pellets, alfalfa cubes, wheat bran, carrots, sweet potatoes, bread, pineapple tops and fruits such as apples.
He said hay played a key role in maintaining digestive health and supporting natural foraging behaviour.
"Apples are included as part of the daily diet and used in controlled quantities as treats during training and enrichment activities.
"They are cut into smaller pieces to facilitate feeding while ensuring the rewards do not affect the balance of their diet," he said.
Previously, Kelat was reported to have shown
signs of musth
, including increased activity and a weight loss of about 220kg.
Akmal said the council had complied with all laws, agreements, standard operating procedures and animal welfare guidelines throughout the collaboration with Tennoji Zoo.
He said that the council had extended full cooperation to the relevant authorities in any review, investigation or information requests related to the programme, as part of its commitment to transparency and accountability.
While acknowledging public concern, he urged that any allegations on animal welfare be based on facts, credible information and professional assessments.
He said unverified claims could lead to misunderstandings and undermine Malaysia's conservation efforts and international cooperation.
"The council remains committed to ensuring the welfare, health and well-being of the elephants remain our top priority, and we will continue to provide updates from time to time," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dewan Rakyat defers Competition Bill committee stage to Monday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Dewan Rakyat has deferred the committee stage of the Competition (Amendment) Bill 2026 to Monday to allow further review of the proposed amendments.
Winding up the debate, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the amendments were aimed at strengthening the enforcement capacity of the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) in tackling anti-competitive practices, including cartels and abuse of dominant positions.
He said government-linked companies (GLCs) might be classified as government entities if they acted on delegated authority or carried out functions on behalf of the federal, state or local governments.
"Secondly, GLCs were also raised – specifically, whether they can be considered government entities. Yes, if the GLC acts on delegated authority from the government or carries out functions on behalf of the federal government, state government or local authority, then it may fall within the definition of a government entity.
"Examples include the Armed Forces Fund Board and Perbadanan Nasional Berhad (PNB)," he told the Dewan Rakyat.
On the expanded definition of "enterprise", Armizan said the existing scope was too narrow as it only covered direct buying and selling activities.
He said the broader definition would allow MyCC to investigate trade associations making collective decisions that affect market openness, even if they did not engage directly in commercial activity.
On the new Section 11A empowering MyCC to compel information for market reviews, he said it was necessary due to operational constraints.
"Among the main challenges are difficulty in obtaining credible information, reluctance to cooperate, delays in data submission and the risk of false or misleading information being provided," he said.
Responding to concerns over the up to 40 per cent settlement discount under Section 38A, Armizan said it would not be applied automatically and would require a collective decision by the Commission.
On the absence of merger control provisions, he said it was a deliberate sequencing decision, with the framework to be implemented within the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) period.
The Dewan Rakyat will reconvene on July 6 for the committee stage of the Bill, followed by the second reading.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Paris Baguette Malaysia goes fully under Singapore HQ
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Paris Baguette Malaysia has come under the full ownership of Paris Baguette's Southeast Asia regional headquarters in Singapore following Berjaya Food Bhd's exit.
The company said the restructuring aligns Malaysia more closely with Paris Baguette's broader Southeast Asia growth strategy and reflects its long-term commitment to the local market.
"Supporting the move is Paris Baguette's fully halal-certified production hub in Johor, which serves as a strategic manufacturing base to strengthen supply chain resilience, ensure product consistency and support future expansion across the region," it said.
The company said it will also intensify investments in staff training, quality assurance and customer service to enhance the overall bakery cafe experience.
It noted that Malaysia remains a key market in its regional growth plans, with the company seeking to leverage its global expertise alongside local market insights to build long-term value.
In addition, Paris Baguette also identified halal certification as one of its top strategic priorities, following recent halal certifications obtained in Singapore and Indonesia.
It said advancing halal certification in Malaysia is a non-negotiable commitment, underpinned by the certified supply chain at its Johor halal food hub.
"Halal certification is fundamentally about earning and maintaining consumer trust.
"It is a key pillar of our long-term strategy and positions us for sustainable growth while preserving the quality and craftsmanship that define the Paris Baguette brand," it said.
Paris Baguette said Malaysian customers can expect a wider range of innovative bakery products, an enhanced cafe experience and offerings that combine the brand's international expertise with local tastes and preferences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cathay Pacific to resume Middle East flights
Article:
HONG KONG: Hong Kong's flagship airline Cathay Pacific announced Thursday that it will resume flights to the Middle East, as the United States and Iran signal that efforts to end the war remain viable.
Following the two sides' indirect discussions in Doha on Wednesday, US President Donald Trump, as well as mediators Qatar and Pakistan, offered signs that diplomacy was holding, despite exchanges of fire this week.
Cathay suspended flights to Dubai and Riyadh at the end of February after the war broke out.
The airline has raised fuel surcharges several times as the conflict has driven up oil prices.
It said Thursday it would resume daily passenger flights to Dubai and four-times-weekly passenger flights to Riyadh from Sept 1.
Freighter services to Riyadh will resume from Aug 1.
"Cathay will continue to closely monitor the evolving situation in the Middle East prior to the resumption dates," the company said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HR Ministry launches five-year plan to take Malaysian TVET global
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry today unveiled the Department of Skills Development Internationalisation Action Plan 2026-2030 and the SkillsMalaysia Global initiative to raise the standards and international recognition of Malaysia's Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) system.
Launching the roadmap, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the initiatives would align the National Occupational Skills Standards with international benchmarks while strengthening global recognition of the Malaysian Skills Certificate.
Describing the initiatives as a "game changer", he said they would help Malaysia overcome future economic challenges.
"We are not only preparing local talent to compete internationally, but also ensuring our training system is recognised, competitive and respected globally," he said at the National TVET Instructors and Accredited Centre Managers Conference 2026 today.
Ramanan said the initiatives were in line with the National TVET Council's mandate announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on June 5 to align graduates with high-growth, high-value (HGHV) industries through greater industry participation, advanced training and lifelong learning.
He said achieving this agenda would require a stronger industry-led ecosystem to ensure TVET remained responsive to labour market needs.
The ministry also exchanged memoranda of understanding (MoUs) with Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) and Belia Mahir International TVET Sdn Bhd to strengthen industry collaboration and enhance training programmes aligned with industry needs at the national and regional levels.
Ramanan said the Department of Skills Development's Strategic Plan 2026-2030 would accelerate digital transformation through initiatives including an AI Smart Office, a TVET Data War Room and the development of 150 AI-skilled officers.
He also stressed that artificial intelligence should be viewed as an opportunity rather than a threat.
"Some are worried that AI will take human jobs. I, however, believe that AI will not replace humans; there is no substitute for human capital," he said.
Ramanan said training institutions under the Human Resources Ministry recorded a graduate employability rate of 95.5 per cent within six months of graduation, with 85.1 per cent securing jobs related to their training. Employer satisfaction, meanwhile, stood at 97.2 per cent.
He also urged Malaysians to take advantage of the more than 100,000 vacancies available through the MyFutureJobs portal, noting that the country's unemployment rate had fallen to a record low of 2.9 per cent.
"There are 300,000 job opportunities there, but only 200,000 have been taken. There are still 100,000 vacancies.
"Our unemployment rate is the lowest in history at only 2.9 per cent. In the MyFutureJobs portal, the system uses AI to show you jobs that suit your skills," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: KJ urges Bersatu to follow Pas in backing BN
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin has called on Bersatu members and supporters to follow Pas's example and back Barisan Nasional candidates in seats where Perikatan Nasional is not contesting in the Johor polls.
Khairy, who is also a former Rembau member of parliament, described his call as part of a broader spirit of Malay political cooperation.
He said while BN continues efforts to strengthen support among non-Malay voters, the agenda of Malay unity should not be overlooked, especially in Johor.
"I am also encouraging my friends in Bersatu to follow Pas's approach of supporting BN candidates in seats they are not contesting. For example, here in Parit Yaani, we are encouraging them to support us.
"The Malays in Johor want a united Bangsa Johor based on that spirit. If there is no PN candidate in a seat, we are also encouraging Bersatu voters to support BN.
"We all come from the same roots, so we should be united, not divided. Pas is not our enemy, Bersatu is not our enemy. BN has no enemies with anyone.
"When our friends from Pas and Bersatu are not contesting, we encourage them to support us," he said after the "Lunch Hour With KJ vs NS" programme in Parit Besar here today.
Also present was BN candidate for Parit Yaani Datuk Najib Samuri.
When asked whether he was prepared to engage in formal discussions on the matter, Khairy, fondly known as Kj, said he was not in a position to do so as he is now only an ordinary party member.
"I am just an ordinary member, but I believe there are colleagues discussing this matter regarding Bersatu and Pas.
"When there is no Bersatu candidate, voters will look at the ballot paper between BN and PH (Pakatan Harapan) and decide which they are more comfortable with," he said.
It was previously reported that Pas had called on its supporters, as well as those of PN, to not vote for Pakatan Harapan in seats the federal opposition bloc is not contesting in this state election.
Pas said this also applies to all the seats that are seeing a straight fight between PH and BN.
However, earlier today, a Bersatu leader said the party does not support either PH or BN in this election, which has been fixed for polling on July 11.
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz had said that Bersatu members who
threw their support behind parties other than PN
were committing an act tantamount to sabotage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Malaysia's unemployment rate drops to 2.9 per cent
Article:
LABIS: The country's unemployment rate recorded a decline last year to 2.9 per cent compared with 3.2 per cent in 2024, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid said the matter was informed by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during yesterday's cabinet meeting.
He said the lower unemployment rate reflected the availability of more job opportunities, driven by the government's continued efforts in emphasising skills talent development through Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET).
"In the past, there was unemployment. Now there are job opportunities, many of them in technical fields where training is provided by TVET institutions.
"This shows that our efforts to develop skilled manpower in technical fields have borne fruit," he said when officiating the Johor-level Community Development Department (Kemas) Skills Day at Dataran Labis here today.
Also present were Kemas director-general Datuk Mohd Hanafiah Man, BN candidate for Bekok Tan Chong and BN candidate Tenang Mohd Azahar Ibrahim.
Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said the employability rate among TVET graduates under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) institutions was also excellent, at 99.5 per cent.
"Besides TVET institutions, Kemas has also produced many skilled workers through sewing, barbering, cooking, grooming, make-up and computer classes," he said.
He also encouraged TVET graduates to venture into entrepreneurship.
"These statistics and achievements are the result of our commitment through the ministry, relevant departments, agencies and subsidiary companies in carrying out planning for the welfare of the people sincerely and diligently," he said.
Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said he has instructed Kemas director-general Datuk Mohd Hanafiah Man to prepare a paper for the cabinet, outlining Kemas' achievements for presentation to federal ministers.
"I have instructed him to prepare a paper for the cabinet for me.
"I will present it to the other ministers to show that Kemas has created entrepreneurs among rural communities under its administration," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lawyer Ang denies defaming Loke, vows to fight suit
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Lawyer Ang Woei Shang has denied allegations by DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke that he defamed him, vowing to contest the lawsuit and considering a counterclaim.
In a statement made available to the New Straits Times, Ang maintained that the statements complained of were fair comment based on objective facts and said he would defend the action vigorously.
The suit filed by Loke arose from three videos published on Ang's Facebook page between Feb 26 and May 8, touching on issues including the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC), pork supply, the alleged "corporate mafia" controversy, DAP's relationship with Umno and the Selangor pig farming issue.
Loke filed the suit at the High Court here last week.
Ang said the criticisms were supported by factual materials and news reports presented in the videos, denying they were baseless allegations or malicious attacks.
"Many of my comments are directed at DAP's policies and political direction rather than Loke personally, and therefore do not satisfy the legal elements required to establish defamation," he said.
Ang also disputed Loke's reliance on Section 114A of the Evidence Act 1950 in relation to comments posted by Facebook users under his posts, arguing that the provision did not apply to independent third-party comments and that most of the comments concerned DAP rather than Loke personally.
"It is inconsistent for DAP to invoke Section 114A after the party had previously criticised the provision as oppressive and harmful to freedom of expression," he added.
Ang said he learnt of the suit before being formally served after a fellow lawyer discovered the case had been fixed for mention.
He said he subsequently disclosed the matter during a livestream and publicly urged Loke's lawyers to serve the court documents without delay.
Ang also claimed he had sent Loke a WhatsApp message informing him of the matter but received no response before the writ and statement of claim were served.
He said his legal team had been assembled and dismissed online speculation that he was unwilling to appear in court.
Ang also said he had neither launched nor authorised any public fundraising campaign, adding that he had sufficient financial resources to defend the suit and urging the public not to fall victim to scams claiming to collect funds on his behalf.
It was reported earlier today that Loke had filed a defamation suit in the High Court here, alleging that Ang published three defamatory Facebook videos between Feb 26 and May 8 linking the Seremban member of parliament to the pig farming issue and the alleged corporate mafia.
Loke is seeking damages and a public apology.
The High Court has fixed July 13 for case management.
The New Straits Times has contacted Loke for comment but had not received a response at the time of publication.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia's Aceh flogs couple for kissing in TikTok video
Article:
BANDA ACEH: Officials in Indonesia's staunchly Islamic Aceh province publicly flogged an unmarried couple on Thursday, an AFP reporter witnessed, after the pair kissed in a video posted to social media.
The man and woman, whose ages were not disclosed, both received 21 cane lashes in a public park in the provincial capital Banda Aceh.
Dozens of onlookers watched the caning, with some calling for the officials who carried out the punishment to "whip stronger", an AFP reporter saw.
The couple was reported to the authorities after they were seen kissing during a livestream on the video app TikTok, which prompted an investigation that led to their  punishment, the city's syariah police head Muhammad Rizal told reporters on Thursday.
"They clearly violated the Islamic syariah," Rizal said, adding that it was the first time in the religiously conservative province that people were punished for violating syariah law through social media.
Acts of physical intimacy such as kissing, hugging or touching between men and women who are not husband and wife are forbidden in Aceh, the only province in the Southeast Asian country that applies a version of the Islamic law.
Amnesty International Indonesia spokesman Haeril Halim said the punishment for the couple was "a horrifying act of discrimination."
"This shows that the Aceh Islamic Criminal Code (Qanun Jinayat) has expanded its reach to target peaceful expressions in the digital sphere with the caning of the young couple," he said in a statement to AFP.
He called for the government to end the use of caning, adding that corporal punishment "has no place in a just and humane society."
Another couple also received 27 lashes on Thursday for physical intimacy, while two men were caned 29 times and eight times, respectively, for gambling.
In May, authorities flogged two men and two women 100 times for having sex outside of marriage.
Five others were also caned at the time for violations including being in proximity of members of the opposite sex and alcohol consumption.
Caning, though not a legal form of punishment in Indonesia, retains strong support in Aceh.
Indonesia is the world's largest Muslim-majority country, but officially recognises six religions as well as indigenous beliefs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia to move away from collateral-based financing, says Liew
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs to develop alternative financing models that rely less on physical collateral and better support technology-based businesses, says Deputy Finance Minister Datuk Seri Liew Chin Tong.
He said discussions were ongoing with Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP), Credit Guarantee Corporation (CGC) and Bank Negara Malaysia on new financing approaches, although the matter remained at an early stage.
"We are still very early in the discussion stage but nonetheless we welcome new ideas and possibilities to move away from these physical collateral requirements.
"We are looking at ways to improve alternative borrowing models and credit reporting," he said after delivering the opening keynote at the sixth National Economic Forum (NEF 2026) today.
The forum, organised by the National Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Malaysia (NCCIM), carried the theme A World in Transition: Securing Malaysia's Economic Future in an Era of Disruption.
It drew nearly 600 participants, including business leaders, policymakers, diplomats, economists, academics, investors and industry representatives.
Liew's keynote address, titled Securing Malaysia's Future in a World in Transition: Strengthening Resilience, Confidence and Competitiveness, set the tone for discussions on Malaysia's long-term economic priorities amid global uncertainty.
The forum also featured a keynote presentation by Malaysian Inclusive Development and Advancement Institute (Minda) director Professor Tan Sri Dr Noor Azlan Ghazali, alongside three plenary sessions covering institutional resilience, workforce readiness, geopolitical developments, trade realignment, investment competitiveness and innovation.
Liew said industry proposals to strengthen non-traditional credit assessment would be welcomed.
"If there are suggestions on improving alternative borrowing and credit reporting, we are happy to study them because this is an area we are actively working on," he said.
Responding to a question on intellectual property (IP)-based financing, he said Malaysia must move beyond conventional collateral-backed lending.
"There is a need to shift away from purely collateral-based lending towards financing models that recognise intellectual property and innovation," he added.
Liew said that Malaysia must also strengthen its private equity and venture capital ecosystem to support technology firms.
"In more technologically advanced economies, venture capital plays an important role in financing innovation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz: Beware of candidates promising 'moon and stars'
Article:
KULAI: Voters, especially the youth, have been reminded not to be easily swayed by those promising the "moon and the stars" and preaching rhetoric aimed at undermining the Barisan Nasional administration.
Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also state BN chairman, urged the people to learn from past experiences where they were offered various pledges that remain unfulfilled to this day.
He cited the experience of Simpang Renggam voters, claiming the constituency's former member of parliament made numerous promises but failed to deliver them, before eventually moving to contest in another state seat in these Johor polls.
"In the 2018 General Election, sweet promises were made by the other side just to win votes. In my own constituency of Simpang Renggam, voters were promised free tolls—did they deliver on that promise or not?
"The people of Simpang Renggam said 'heads were soaked' (they were taken for a ride). They were promised the moon and stars. Besides free tolls, what else? PTPTN (National Higher Education Fund Corporation loans)? Has it been settled yet? What else did they promise?
"Today, he has run off to another constituency. Where? He ran off to Puteri Wangsa," he said during his speech at a community gathering with the Kulai society at the Dataran Flat Cengal in Taman Putri here.
Also present was Umno supreme council member Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican.
Onn Hafiz said there were candidates were also resorting to instigation to mislead the public.
"Recently, he instigated the folks over there (Puteri Wangsa) by claiming that roads in Johor are riddled with potholes. However, the roads he was referring to are federal roads, not state roads.
"That is their MO (modus operandi). That is how they campaign… Promising the moon and stars while instigating the people.
"It is the same case here. What did Arthur (Bukit Batu incumbent assemblyman Arthur Chiong Sen Sern) say? He claimed he did not receive allocations from the state government. But a recording surfaced—in this digital world, whatever we say is recorded," he said.
Onn Hafiz reminded all candidates to keep their speeches brief during the election campaign to avoid misleading the public.
"That is why I remind politicians not to talk too much. Keep your speeches short during campaigns. Sometimes when they get carried away, that is when they resort to deceiving the people," he said.
He also reminded the BN machinery that they bear a heavy responsibility to ensure voters, particularly the younger demographic, cast their ballots for the coalition.
"Back in 2018, some of our children living in KL (Kuala Lumpur) or working in other states came back to their hometowns to vote for the opposition.
"Not only that, they coaxed their parents into supporting the other side too because they had been promised the moon and stars.
"Some fell for it, and parents followed suit. As such, please ensure that we engage with the young voters in our respective areas.
"To all machinery members, you carry a heavy responsibility," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor contributes 26.5pct to Malaysia's GDP, widening lead over KL and Johor
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Selangor recorded strong economic growth in 2025 with its gross domestic product (GDP) expanding by RM28 billion from the preceding year to hit RM460.1 billion.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the growth in economic value was no less than twice of any other state in the country based on data released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) yesterday.
According to DOSM, Selangor was the main contributor to the national economy, followed by Kuala Lumpur whose GDP grew by RM13.2 billion to RM265.1 billion.
Amirudin said the achievement also surpassed an economic forecast conducted by Universiti Putra Malaysia in collaboration with the Selangor Research Institute, which had projected Selangor's economy would grow to about RM455.3 billion.
"The growth has increased Selangor's contribution to Malaysia's GDP from 26.2 per cent to 26.5 per cent, expanding the state's economy to more than 1.7 times the size of Kuala Lumpur's and 2.7 times that of Johor's," he said.
Amirudin also said Selangor's economic growth of 6.3 per cent in 2025 outperformed Malaysia's overall economic growth rate of 5.2 per cent.
The growth was driven by three key sectors: services, which contributed RM15.9 billion; manufacturing (RM5.3 billion); and construction (RM3.7 billion).
He said the achievement further strengthened Selangor's position, noting that the state accounted for 35.9 per cent of the country's construction activity, while its share of the national manufacturing sector increased to 32.8 per cent and its contribution to the services sector rose to 27.1 per cent.
According to him, such growth would not have been possible without the restructuring of the state's economy through the First Selangor Plan (RS-1), the state's five-year socioeconomic development plan covering the 2021-2025 period.
"Over the RS-1 period, Selangor's economy expanded by 33.94 per cent, or more than one-third – equivalent to RM116.6 billion – rising from RM343.5 billion before the plan began to RM460.1 billion in 2025," he said.
In the statement, Amirudin expressed his appreciation to the civil service, industry players, and the people of Selangor for their commitment and contributions to the state's economic success.
Despite the achievement, all parties must not become complacent but instead rise to the challenge of making Selangor the first state in Malaysia to achieve a RM500 billion economy, he said, calling for continued cooperation and higher productivity from both the public and private sectors.
"Moving forward, this administration will focus on improving the people's standard of living so that the state's economic progress continues to deliver positive improvements to their quality of life," he added.
Meanwhile, Invest Selangor said in a statement that the excellent performance represented significant momentum from the RM406.1 billion recorded in 2023, making Selangor the first state to surpass the RM400 billion mark for two consecutive years.
According to the agency, the encouraging performance further reinforced the state's role as the country's largest contributor to the national economy, accounting for 26.2 per cent of Malaysia's total GDP, up from 25.9 per cent in the previous year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: What does it mean to be excommunicated from the Catholic Church?
Article:
THE Vatican said on Thursday that priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval were in schism with the wider Church and now excommunicated.
Here is background on what excommunication from the 1.4-billion-member Catholic Church means for the ultra-traditionalist Society of St. Pius X.
What is excommunication?
Excommunication, deriving from Latin words meaning to be "out of communion" with the Church, is one of the most serious penalties that can be imposed by the Vatican.
It bars a Catholic from receiving sacraments, getting married, or holding Church office, among other measures.
The Church considers excommunication as a "medicinal" penalty. It is not forever. It is a temporary measure that can be removed when the person at fault repents of their action and asks for forgiveness.
What is schism?
Schism is a term to indicate a severe, formal rupture inside the Catholic community. It indicates that a person or group has broken their relationship with the pope, who Catholics believe is a successor to St. Peter, one of Jesus' 12 apostles.
Why were these people excommunicated?
It is a strict teaching of the Church that only the pope can authorise the consecration of new bishops in order to maintain the Church's ties to the ​apostles, who are considered the first priests and bishops.
Consecration without papal consent incurs automatic excommunication for both the person being ​consecrated and the bishop conducting the ceremony.
What is automatic excommunication?
The Church considers some actions so serious that they impose an automatic excommunication, known by the Latin term "latae sententiae."
This means that the excommunication happens at the moment of the action, without need for any decree from the Vatican.
Sometimes, however, the Vatican will make a public announcement to inform Catholics of the penalty.
Other offences of this type include a priest who violates the seal of confession or a Catholic who physically attacks the pope.
A recent high-profile automatic excommunication was handed down in 2024 to Archbishop Carlo Maria Vigano, a former Vatican ambassador to the United States and critic of Pope Francis.
The Vatican said he had refused to recognise and submit to the pope.
Who are the Society of St Pius X?
They are an ultra-traditionalist group that denies the ​key teachings of the Second Vatican Council, a landmark Vatican gathering of bishops in the 1960s that pursued a range of reforms for the global Church and sought to repair its relations with Jews and other Christian denominations.
The group, which counts about 733 priests worldwide, has had tense relations with the Vatican for decades.
Its late founder, Archbishop ​Marcel Lefebvre, was excommunicated in 1988 after ordaining four bishops without permission from then-Pope John Paul II.
Benedict XVI, John Paul's successor, sought to renew dialogue with the society and lifted the ​remaining excommunications of four bishops.
That action sparked a fierce backlash from Jewish leaders when it became known that one of those bishops, Richard Williamson, had publicly denied the extent of the Holocaust.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Job seeker says identity used in RM2.4mil scam syndicate
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 26-year-old man who believed he had secured a customer service job claims he was duped into opening bank accounts later used by a scam syndicate to move RM2.4 million.
The victim, known only as Jay, said he was instructed to open a bank account during a week-long training programme, purportedly for salary payments.
"They offered accommodation and meals and said I had to undergo a week of training. During that period, they asked me to open a bank account, supposedly for salary purposes," he said at a press conference today.
Jay said he was later told his job application had been unsuccessful before being taken to several other banks to open more accounts.
Growing suspicious after three banks allegedly rejected the applications, he wanted to withdraw but claimed he was persuaded to continue.
The training ended in late April, after which he waited to be contacted about starting work.
"A few days later, everyone disappeared. The WhatsApp messages showed only one tick. I tried to trace those involved and discovered that a company had been registered under my name, listing me as the owner.
"When I checked with the Companies Commission of Malaysia, I contacted the company secretary, who told me he had also been detained over a similar case," he said.
Further checks revealed his identity had allegedly been used to register two internet service accounts, with cancellation penalties totalling about RM14,000.
Jay also discovered several bank accounts had been opened in his name, including at banks he claimed never to have visited.
"I later found out my name was also used to register five mobile phone numbers. Those numbers were then used to scam other people," he said.
Jay admitted he did not suspect any wrongdoing because the training appeared legitimate and participants were housed together.
Meanwhile, Malaysian International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) secretary-general Datuk Hishammuddin Hashim said the organisation would assist Jay in lodging a police report.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lim, Pua respond to Sultan: LRT3 costs RM9.72bil less
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Shah Alam LRT3 project was ultimately completed at a cost of RM21.93 billion — RM9.72 billion lower than the RM31.65 billion originally approved during the previous Barisan Nasional administration.
The savings were the result of cost rationalisation undertaken by the Pakatan Harapan (PH) government, former finance minister Lim Guan Eng and his former political secretary Tony Pua said today.
In a joint statement issued in response to
Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah's remarks
on the project yesterday, Lim and Pua said the savings were achieved without compromising passenger capacity or the rail line's functionality.
They said the lower project cost would also reduce the government's debt burden, resulting in anticipated interest savings of up to RM10 billion over 25 to 30 years.
"We would like to reiterate the fact that these savings to the Malaysian government were not at the expense of the LRT passenger volume and functionality.
"These savings in turn allowed the current and future governments to reallocate funds to other welfare benefits for the rakyat and development expenditure," they said.
Lim and Pua also disputed claims that the LRT3 project had been stopped or suspended under the PH administration between 2018 and 2020.
They maintained that the project continued throughout PH's tenure, describing it as a critical infrastructure project to improve the daily lives of Klang Valley residents.
According to the statement, the Najib administration had approved RM9 billion for the project in 2015 and provided Prasarana Malaysia Bhd with a RM10 billion government guarantee to raise funds.
However, they said Prasarana subsequently awarded work package contracts worth RM15.2 billion in March 2018, exceeding the original approved allocation.
After factoring in land acquisition, project delivery partner (PDP) and consultancy fees, as well as financing costs, Prasarana sought a revised project budget of RM31.65 billion and an additional RM22 billion government guarantee in March 2018, they said.
Lim and Pua said that after PH took office in May 2018, construction continued while the government worked with project delivery partner MRCB-George Kent to reduce costs.
They said the project was only 9 per cent complete at the time, allowing the government to engage independent engineering consultants to review the project and recommend cost-saving measures.
Following the review, the Cabinet approved reducing the project cost from RM31.65 billion to RM16.63 billion, representing savings of RM15.02 billion.
Among the measures taken were reducing the train fleet from 42 six-car train sets to 22 three-car train sets based on passenger demand studies approved by the then Land Public Transport Commission (SPAD), deferring five stations with low projected initial ridership, and replacing the PDP model with a fixed-price contract to prevent cost overruns.
The five deferred stations have since been reinstated following further development in the surrounding areas.
Lim and Pua said the government later awarded a RM5.3 billion Phase 2 contract covering the construction of the stations, additional rolling stock and other upgrades, bringing the final project cost to RM21.93 billion.
They also defended the decision to extend the project's completion target from 2020 to 2024, saying the original deadline was unrealistic as construction was only 9 per cent complete by mid-2018.
Yesterday, Sultan Sharafuddin said the then finance minister Lim and his adviser Pua had scaled down the project in 2018 because they viewed it as a "mega project" and did not fully appreciate its purpose of serving the public.
The ruler also credited former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak for approving the project after a request from the palace, while thanking Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for ensuring its continuation and completion, including reinstating five stations previously removed from the project scope.
In their statement today, Lim and Pua expressed appreciation for the Sultan's concern over the LRT3 project and agreed that it plays an important role in easing traffic congestion, reducing travel time and serving as an economic catalyst for Selangor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MyLesen B2 assistance not a ticket for illegal street racing, warns Anthony Loke
Article:
SEREMBAN: Youth and students who benefit from the MyLesen B2 programme must not view the assistance as a ticket to participate in illegal street racing.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative under the Madani government aims to provide low-income families and youth with the opportunity to obtain valid motorcycle licences safely and affordably.
"We want the recipients of the MyLesen B2 programme to use it responsibly for their benefit, and not treat it as a license to engage in unhealthy activities such as illegal racing," he told reporters after launching the state-level MyLesen B2 presentation ceremony at the Tuanku Muhammad Broadcasting Complex auditorium today.
Also present were Transport Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Jana Santhiran Muniyan and Road Transport Department (RTD) director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.
Loke, who is also the Seremban member of parliament, revealed that the government is intensifying enforcement against street racers.
Under a proposed legal framework, individuals caught racing in groups of more than two will face criminal charges in court instead of receiving standard compound fines.
"This new law will be brought to the Dewan Negara next month before being gazetted. However, the ministry and RTD will continue to enhance advocacy and education, as road safety does not rely solely on legislation," he said.
He noted that around two-thirds of nationwide road accidents involve motorcyclists, and the target demographic for this safety-oriented licensing programme includes secondary school students and youths.
Since its inception in 2023, approximately 150,000 participants nationwide have benefited from the programme.
In Negri Sembilan, the quota for the MyLesen B2 initiative was increased to 2,300 participants this year, compared to 1,000 participants in 2025.
The programme received 2,929 applications in the state, with 2,315 applicants verified as eligible.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar congratulates China on 105th anniversary of ruling party
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated the Chinese government on the 105th anniversary of the Chinese ruling party.
In a Facebook post today, Anwar said he had sent a letter to Chinese President Xi Jinping, conveying congratulations from the Malaysian government and its people.
The prime minister expressed his appreciation for the long journey of the Chinese ruling party, noting that it has shaped the country's progress based on the strength of its civilisation, history, and long-inherited values.
"I also recall Malaysia's historic decision on May 31, 1974, as the first non-socialist country in Southeast Asia to recognise the People's Republic of China.
"The decision, made with great wisdom and foresight, continues to be a solid foundation for the close relationship that has been strengthened for over five decades," Anwar said.
He reiterated Malaysia's commitment to continue deepening strategic cooperation with China based on respect, trust, and shared benefits for the sake of regional peace, stability, and prosperity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Coffee shop operator recounts escape from Penang shoplot fire
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A coffee shop operator recounted a terrifying escape after a fire destroyed two three-storey shoplots in Jalan Perak here today, while a trapped foreign worker was rescued unharmed.
Chang Sow King, 55, said she and another worker detected a burning smell before discovering the blaze.
"I smelled something burning. When I looked, there was indeed a fire. We immediately alerted everyone and told them to get out because we feared it would spread," she said at the scene.
Chang said the fire spread rapidly, leaving them with no time to save their belongings.
"I was really frightened. The flames spread very quickly and we ran outside to wait for the firefighters. Thankfully, my Nepalese worker, who was initially trapped, was rescued safely," she said.
Earlier, it was reported that a man was
rescued
unharmed after the fire gutted two three-storey shoplots housing a coffee shop.
The fire, reported at 12.32pm, damaged the first shoplot by about 90 per cent and the second by about 80 per cent.
The blaze was brought under control at 12.44pm.
The victim, believed to be in his 30s or 40s, was rescued by firefighters and was unhurt.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Marine Police identify new tactic as traffickers use female lookouts
Article:
TUMPAT: Human trafficking syndicates are increasingly recruiting local women as lookouts to monitor enforcement personnel along the Malaysia-Thailand border.
Region Three Marine Police commander Assistant Commissioner Mohd Khairuddin Jamal said this was among the new tactics used by syndicates to evade detection and facilitate the smuggling of undocumented migrants.
"We have identified the routes used by undocumented migrants to enter the country, and we also know the syndicates' modus operandi. Our priority is to target illegal migrant entry points and cripple the syndicates," he said.
Khairuddin said the Marine Police had identified more than 10 smuggling hotspots along the Kelantan-Thailand border.
He recently met officers and intelligence personnel at the Pengkalan Kubor Marine Police base to identify priority targets and coordinate operations.
"Our operations are now focusing on dismantling the criminal networks behind the illegal cross-border activities. Our personnel have been deployed to these areas over the past three months," he said.
He noted that a major challenge remains the presence of lookouts, locally known as tonto, who constantly track enforcement movements.
"They employ various methods to gather information, including using local women as lookouts," he said, adding that locals also act as guides, transporters, and protectors linked to regional criminal networks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aiman Athirah: RM278.9mil allocated for fire and rescue projects nationwide
Article:
KUANTAN: The Fire and Rescue Department has been allocated RM278.9 million under the First Rolling Plan of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), covering 27 new projects and 59 ongoing developments nationwide.
The Housing and Local Government Ministry has identified new fire stations in Tanjung Lumpur and Gambang among several projects under the Second Rolling Plan of the 13MP, as it expands fire and rescue infrastructure in Pahang.
Deputy minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said proposals had been submitted for additional development, including the Gambang station and quarters, the Tanjung Lumpur station, and the reconstruction of the Jerantut fire station.
[[nid:1478737]]
"Under the 12th Malaysia Plan and the First Rolling Plan of the 13MP, seven projects are being implemented in Pahang, including the acquisition of the Gohtong Jaya Fire Station and quarters, the new Benta Fire and Rescue Station in Lipis, and the Tioman Fire Station and quarters in Rompin," she said in her speech at the opening of the Sungai Lembing Fire and Rescue Station here today.
"Other projects include upgrading works for the Bentong and Kuantan fire stations, reconstruction of the Triang station, and preliminary site works for the Tanjung Lumpur station," she added.
The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, officiated the RM6 million
Sungai Lembing fire station here today.
[[nid:1478736]]
Aiman said the investments aim to strengthen fire and rescue services through improved infrastructure, equipment and personnel capabilities.
The Sungai Lembing station, built on 2.8ha, will serve about 10,000 residents in the former tin mining town.
Previously, emergencies in the area were handled by the Indera Mahkota fire station, about 30km away.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elephant calf killed in Mersing crash, mother relocated to safety
Article:
MERSING: A five-year-old female elephant calf was killed after being struck by a vehicle yesterday, while an adult elephant believed to be its mother was relocated to a sanctuary.
The Johor Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) received a report on
the incident
at 8.30am and immediately deployed personnel to investigate.
Caretaker Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the calf, identified as part of the Jamaluang-Mersing herd, died at the scene while the adult elephant remained nearby.
To ensure public safety and the animal's welfare, the Johor Elephant Sanctuary (JES) Elephant Capture Unit and Perhilitan relocated the adult elephant to a safer location.
The calf's carcass was buried in accordance with standard procedures, he said in a statement today.
Ling said Perhilitan would continue monitoring and patrolling the area to prevent similar incidents.
[[nid:1478780]]
He urged motorists to remain vigilant when travelling through wildlife habitats, especially at night and along identified wildlife corridors.
Ling said the state government's RM66 million wildlife crossing project along Jalan Kahang-Mersing remains under construction.
The 1.2km crossing features an 8m-high, 200m-long viaduct to allow wildlife to pass safely beneath the road.
The project had reached 10.12 per cent completion as of June 29 and is expected to be completed by Feb 7, 2028.
"I hope the completion of this project will reduce wildlife roadkill," he said.
Ling added that the Johor government remained committed to balancing development, public safety and wildlife conservation.
The incident
comes just over a year after a five-year-old male elephant calf was killed after being hit by a poultry lorry on the Gerik-Jeli East-West Highway, where its mother remained beside the carcass for hours in a scene that drew nationwide attention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fugitive Jho Low reportedly considered for US presidential pardon by Trump
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho could be slated for a presidential pardon from United States President Donald Trump as the country celebrates its 250th Independence Day on July 4.
Media organisation The Atlantic reported that Low, widely known as Jho Low, could be among 250 individuals Trump might consider granting clemency.
Low faces multiple charges, including corruption and money laundering in the US and Malaysia, for his alleged role in the misappropriation of at least US$4.5 billion (RM18.3 billion) from 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB).
He has consistently denied any wrongdoing, and his whereabouts remain unknown.
The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported that Low filed a pardon request in May to dismiss his US criminal charges.
The US Justice Department website lists a
pending request for a "Pardon after Completion of Sentence" under Taek Jho Low
filed this year.
However, a White House official stated that Low's request was not currently on their radar.
The WSJ also reported that Malaysia had temporarily lifted an Interpol red notice against Low to facilitate the return of significant assets to the country.
Rapper Pras Michel, a founding member of the Fugees who was convicted over an illegal lobbying campaign linked to Low, is also reportedly in line for a pardon.
In Malaysia, Prime Minister Datuk Seri
Anwar Ibrahim previously clarified that the government is not considering any possibility of granting a pardon
to Low.
1MDB Asset Recovery Task Force chairman Datuk Seri
Johari Abdul Ghani also said Low's request should be rejected
, urging the US to instead assist Malaysia in locating him for further investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran chief negotiator calls to avenge Khamenei death with massive funeral turnout
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf called on Thursday for massive turnout at Ali Khamenei's funeral to avenge the supreme leader's death in US-Israeli strikes at the start of the war.
"I invite all the Iranian people... to write a glorious page in the history of Islamic Iran through your presence" at the funeral ceremonies starting Saturday, said Ghalibaf, who is also Iran's parliament speaker.
"The nation's call for vengeance must ring in the ears of the whole world," he added in a statement.
Khamenei's funeral, initially delayed at the height of the Middle East war, will take place as Iran and the United States observe a fragile ceasefire after signing a preliminary deal to halt the conflict.
Khamenei, a spiritual figure for many Shias, was killed at the age of 86 at his compound in the centre of the Iranian capital on Feb 28, the first day of the war.
His public funeral will begin on Saturday, with his body lying in state at the colossal Grand Mosalla complex in central Teheran that hosts major Friday prayers, official ceremonies and religious gatherings.
The bodies of his slain relatives will also be presented.
[[nid:1478725]]
The ceremonies are expected to draw between 15 and 20 million mourners, according to officials, which would make it the biggest state funeral in the country's history.
"Iran... is preparing to experience one of the most significant moments in its history," Ghalibaf said.
Teheran, as well as the holy cities of Qom and Mashhad, which will host later stages of the funeral and burial ceremonies, will observe public holidays while the events are underway.
Authorities have ordered public and private offices in Teheran to close from Saturday through Monday, while traffic restrictions will make much of the city centre inaccessible to private vehicles.
The airspace over Teheran will be partially closed from Friday and fully closed on Monday.
Following the ceremonies in Teheran, Khamenei's body will be taken to the holy Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala before his burial on July 9 at the shrine of Imam Reza in the northeastern city of Mashhad, his birthplace.
But at the main ceremony in Teheran, it remains unknown if Khamenei's son and successor Mojtaba, who has not been seen in public since becoming supreme leader, will be present.
Representatives from around 30 countries are expected to attend the funeral, with people pouring in from neighbouring Iraq, Afghanistan and Pakistan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JP Morgan stays bullish on Malaysia equities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: JP Morgan remains optimistic on Malaysian equities in the second half of 2026 driven by strong private-sector investment cycle, supportive domestic fund positioning and resilient banking stocks.
JP Morgan said Malaysia's private sector investment cycle continues to strengthen, with fixed asset investment rising to 22 per cent of gross domestic products (GDP).
It noted that the external backdrop remains supportive, with global AI-related capex spending now expected to reach US$5.5 trillion through 2030 (up from US$5.1trillion last Nov) with 138 gigawatts (GW) of DC capacity growth (from 122 GW).
"Based on our estimates, Malaysia's datacentre pipeline has expanded to 13GW, larger than Indonesia/Thailand/Singapore combined, reinforcing Malaysia's position as Asean's leading AI infrastructure market.
"Unlike previous investment cycles led by commodities or government infrastructure projects, the current cycle is increasingly funded by corporates, hyperscalers and multinational companies.
"We believe this provides greater visibility and duration of investment spending, supporting earnings across Utilities, Industrials and selected Technology names," it said.
Meanwhile, JP Morgan said positioning remains supportive, with domestic mutual funds still holding 6.4 per cent of assets under management (AUM) in cash, while foreign ownership is at an historical low of 18.3 per cent.
It noted that banks remain attractive portfolio anchors, underpinned by resilient asset quality, attractive dividend yields and potential capital management upside.
"Banks are unlikely to deliver the fastest earnings growth, in our view, but remain among the market's most attractive risk-adjusted opportunities," it said.
Overall, JP Morgan said Malaysian equities have fared relatively better than Asean during past periods of strong US dollar, driven by resilient earnings, light foreign positions and current account surplus.
It noted that consensus financial year 2026 (FY26)/ FY27 earning per share (EPS) growth remains broadly unchanged at seven/six per cent.
"We therefore expect market leadership to remain concentrated in companies with direct leverage to Malaysia's investment cycle rather than a broad market re-rating."
It maintained its "Overweight" call on Malaysia with a 1,800 KLCI end-2026 target, driven by seven per cent EPS growth and a 14 times forward price-to-earnings (P/E).
JP Morgan identified three potential catalysts that could provide further upside to the Malaysian market.
These include clearer implementation milestones for the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone, increased corporate activity under the MY Value Up initiative and the possible emergence of El Niño.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysians spend over five hours daily on unpaid domestic work, caregiving, says DOSM
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysians spend an average of five hours and 12 minutes a day on unpaid household chores and caregiving, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM).
Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said this represents 21.7 per cent of their day, with adults in their prime working years shouldering the largest share of domestic responsibilities.
Findings from the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025 showed that individuals aged between 45 and 54 devote the most time to unpaid work at five hours and 43 minutes a day.
They are followed closely by the 25 to 44 age group at five hours and 35 minutes, and those aged 55 to 64 at five hours and 31 minutes. In contrast, individuals aged between 15 and 24 spend the least amount of time, averaging three hours and 53 minutes a day.
"This reflects the greater domestic and caregiving responsibilities typically borne by people in the middle stages of life," Uzir said in a statement today.
He added that Malaysians spend more time on unpaid domestic and care work than the average in member countries of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
The survey revealed that Malaysian women spend an average of six hours and seven minutes a day, or 25.5 per cent of their daily time, on unpaid chores, compared with about 17 per cent in OECD countries.
Men devote 18.1 per cent of their day to such work, compared with around nine to 11 per cent internationally.
"These findings show that the burden of unpaid work remains high in Malaysia for both men and women, with women continuing to shoulder a greater share of the responsibility," he said.
The study also noted that persons without disabilities spend five hours and 12 minutes a day on unpaid tasks, compared with two hours and 59 minutes among persons with disabilities.
Uzir said the data provides an important basis for strengthening policies on social development, work-life balance, and gender equality to boost national productivity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Belgium eyes tech transfer over rare earths from Malaysia, seeks deeper mineral cooperation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Belgium seeks to strengthen rare earth mineral ties with Malaysia in its bid to reduce reliance on China, the kingdom’s deputy prime minister Maxime Prévot said today.
However, Prévot stressed that Belgium wants to strengthen technology transfer with Malaysia in the critical mineral sector rather than merely increasing exports of resources.
He said Malaysia could benefit from the expertise of Belgian companies like Solvay and Umicore to efficiently extract different elements within the rare earth supply chain.
“We do not intend to import products coming from the critical minerals in Malaysia because according to the discussion I have had with the authorities, they would like to avoid to export such critical materials,” Prévot told
Malay Mail
in an exclusive interview after attending the 39th Asia-Pacific Roundtable today.
“So, I think that there exists a room for cooperation between the expertise that Belgians have and the needs of the Malaysian authorities on this issue.
“We are certainly not trying to vaporise your assets,” he added.
Malaysia was the third-largest supplier of rare earth elements to the European Union (EU) after China and Russia in 2025, accounting for 23 per cent or 3,500 tonnes of imports, according to Eurostat.
However, Malaysia — also the world’s second-largest exporter of refined rare earth elements after China — bans the export of raw, unprocessed rare earths to protect its domestic resources.
The country is also a key exporter of refined petroleum to Belgium, which amounted to RM317 million in April 2026, according to the Observatory of Economic Complexity (OEC).
Bilateral trade between Malaysia and Belgium totalled RM9.74 billion in 2025, comprising RM6.85 billion in Malaysian exports and RM2.89 billion in imports from Belgium.
Asked if disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz have hurt Belgium’s oil flow, Prévot said the energy shocks has compelled EU countries to diversify their supply chain.
“That is one of the reasons it is really important to be in contact with many countries around the world, and certainly countries from the Asean region, in order to conclude new opportunities.
“It is also important to achieve a peace agreement in order to keep the Strait of Hormuz free and to guarantee respect of international maritime law.
“Like most Asean countries, we (Belgium) are also a maritime nation. So, it is really important to respect such international law,” he explained.
Push for Palestinian statehood
Prévot also described the Belgian government’s decision to recognise the state of Palestine and impose sanctions against Israel — which Malaysia also strongly advocates — as a “coherent and crucial move that aligns with international law.”
“Belgium is a friend of the Jewish community and the Israeli people. We have no problem with the citizens.
“We have problems with the current Israeli government, led by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and the way he is acting.
“And that’s what we are denouncing through a concrete manner and not just by staying at the balcony,” he said.
Belgium formally recognised the Palestinian state at the United Nations General Assembly last year and slapped Israel with 12 sanctions that included an import ban on products from illegal Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank.
Prévot, who also oversees Belgium’s foreign affairs, European affairs and development cooperation portfolio, is on his first working visit to Malaysia since taking office in February 2025.

Title: Households in Malaysia waste up to 97kg of food per person each year, stats show
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — Food waste among households in Malaysia is estimated at between 31.9 kilogrammes (kg) to 97.3kg per capita annually, said Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin.
He said that based on the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025, food waste is more significant for processed or cooked food compared to raw food, with 94.1 per cent of households reported to have thrown away processed or cooked food compared to 88.7 per cent for raw food.
“These findings show that a large portion of food waste occurs after food has been prepared, highlighting the importance of better household practices in terms of meal planning, food preparation and managing leftovers,” he said in a statement today.
He said that 79.3 per cent of households dispose of food together with other household waste, while only 20.7 per cent practise food waste separation.
He added that this showed that food waste separation is still not a common practice among households, thus posing a challenge to efforts to improve the efficiency of organic waste management and reduce reliance on landfill sites.
Mohd Uzir said that going by food type, vegetables were the most wasted category of raw food at 29.1 per cent, followed by fruits (22.4 per cent) and fish or seafood (15 per cent).
As for processed or cooked food, he said rice (16.7 per cent) recorded the highest wastage rate, followed by vegetables (15.8 per cent) and food bought from outside (13.8 per cent).
“This pattern shows that food waste mainly involves fresh food that is perishable as well as staple foods that are commonly prepared in large quantities,” he said.
He said 19.3 per cent of food waste was due to expiration, while other factors included storing leftovers in the refrigerator or freezer for too long (18.1 per cent), over-purchasing (15.2 per cent) and cooking in excessive quantities (15.1 per cent).
“These findings show that food waste is largely influenced by food management practices at the household level, especially in terms of purchasing planning, food storage and portion control in food preparation,” he added.
Mohd Uzir said the pattern of household food waste in Malaysia was generally in line with the global trend of 79kg per capita per year.
However, he said Malaysia has its own local characteristics, influenced by the eating habits of the community, especially the wastage of rice and vegetables
“The information produced will support evidence-based policy formulation and raise public awareness on the importance of more efficient food management practices, thus contributing to the achievement of the country’s sustainable development agenda,” he added. — Bernama

Title: Hardware store worker in Terengganu accused of raping 16‑year‑old sister, pleads not guilty
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU, July 2 — A 25-year-old man was charged at the Sessions Court here today on two counts of rape and physical sexual assault against his 16-year-old biological sister in January and June last year.
The accused, however, pleaded not guilty after both charges were read out separately before Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli.
According to the first charge, the hardware store worker was charged with raping his biological sister at about 11pm in Kampung Titian Baru, here in January.
The charge was framed under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison sentence of between eight and 30 years and whipping of not less than 10 strokes.
For the second charge, he was accused of committing physical sexual assault against the same victim at about 9pm on June 21 at the same location.
The charge was framed under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which provides for imprisonment of up to 20 years and whipping, and an additional prison sentence of up to five years and whipping of not less than two times, if convicted.
The court denied bail and set August 2 for the case mention.
The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Muhammad Asymawi Ghazali while the accused was represented by a lawyer from the National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK). — Bernama

Title: Single mother pleads not guilty to obscene material and altered nude image charges in Melaka
Article:
MELAKA, July 2 — A single mother pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Magistrates’ Court today to possessing obscene material and distributing altered nude images through social media last month.
T. V. Pavithra, 33, entered her plea after the two charges were read out by the court interpreter before Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Sulaiman.
On the first count, she was accused of possessing obscene material on her mobile phone at a condominium in Kota Laksamana Jaya at about 6.15pm on June 28.
The charge was framed under Section 292 of the Penal Code, which provides for imprisonment of up to three years, or a fine, or both upon conviction.
On the second count, she was charged with using social media with intent to distribute an altered nude image of a woman, which was likely to cause harassment, distress, fear, or alarm to the victim.
The accused is alleged to have committed the offence at a house in Taman Melaka Raya at about 11pm on June 24.
The charge, under Section 507B of the Penal Code, carries a maximum jail term of three years, a fine, or both if found guilty.
Deputy public prosecutor Syaza Nur Sharif proposed bail of RM10,000 for each charge, with additional conditions that the accused not disturb the victim, the victim’s family, and prosecution witnesses until the case is concluded.
Lawyer from the National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK) Muhammad Izzuddin Ab Malek, representing Pavithra, applied for bail to be reduced, saying the accused, who earns income solely from an online business, supports her physically disabled mother and mentally disabled younger sibling.
The court allowed bail of RM5,000 for both charges, together with the additional conditions proposed by the deputy public prosecutor, and fixed Aug 5 for mention. — Bernama

Title: Alleged Kembara driver remanded after police arrest couple over viral Federal Highway crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Police have arrested a husband and wife over a viral crash on the Federal Highway last month, with the man believed to have been behind the wheel of a Perodua Kembara now remanded for four days as investigators move closer to wrapping up the case.
Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the pair, both in their 50s, were arrested by the district police’s Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division at about 9pm yesterday.
“A remand application for both suspects was made today. The magistrate granted a four-day remand order for the male suspect until July 5, while his wife was released on police bail after her statement was recorded,” he said in a statement today.
“The investigation paper will be referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor’s office in the near future for a decision on the case.”
The arrests mark the latest development in the investigation into the June 20 incident, which drew widespread attention after footage of the crash circulated online.
Police had previously appealed for help in tracing the driver of the Perodua Kembara, who allegedly fled after driving dangerously and causing the crash on the Federal Highway heading towards Kuala Lumpur.
Earlier investigations found the incident occurred at about 6.10pm as the sport utility vehicle was travelling from Shah Alam towards Kuala Lumpur. The vehicle was allegedly driven by a 59-year-old Malaysian.
Shamsudin urged anyone with information about the incident to contact Petaling Jaya district traffic investigators or visit the nearest police station to assist with the investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, which covers reckless and dangerous driving.

Title: Businessman charged with being member of Geng RT6 organised crime group in Sentul since 2020
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — A businessman was charged in the Sessions Court here today with being a member of an organised crime group known as Geng RT6 since 2020.
S. Ashok Kumar, 50, nodded in understanding after the charge was read out to him before Judge Muhammad Ilmami Ahmad.
No plea was recorded from the accused as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
He is accused of being a member of the Geng RT6 organised crime group in Sentul from Jan 14, 2020 to April 27, 2026.
